label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. There's this problem with 100 lockers, all starting closed. Then there are people from 1 to 100. Each person k toggles every k-th locker. Toggling means if it's open, they close it, and if it's closed, they open it. The question is, how many lockers are open at the end? Hmm, I remember something about this problem before. It might have to do with factors or divisors. Let me think. Each locker is toggled by every person whose number is a factor of the locker number. For example, locker number 6 would be toggled by person 1, 2, 3, and 6. So each time a person's number divides evenly into the locker number, they toggle it. Now, initially, all lockers are closed. So, if a locker is toggled an even number of times, it will end up closed again, since each toggle switches the state. But if it's toggled an odd number of times, it will end up open. So, the key is to figure out how many locker numbers have an odd number of factors. Wait, but most numbers have an even number of factors, right? Because
